names.japanese_name: 亜硫酸水素ナトリウム

natural_occurrence.description: A synthetic inorganic salt that functions as a preservative, antioxidant, and anti-browning agent. Part of the sulfite family (E220-E228) which release sulfur dioxide (SO₂) in food and beverages. Known allergen that can cause severe reactions in sensitive individuals, particularly asthmatics.

manufacturing.description: Produced by passing sulfur dioxide (SO₂) gas through an aqueous solution of sodium carbonate or sodium hydroxide. The resulting solution contains a mixture of sodium bisulfite and sodium metabisulfite in equilibrium. Can also be made by treating sodium sulfite solution with additional sulfur dioxide.

other_uses.industrial: Used in water treatment, textile processing, paper manufacturing, and as a reducing agent in chemical synthesis
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Used in some pharmaceutical formulations as an antioxidant and stabilizer
other_uses.household: Used in home brewing and wine-making

eu.conditions: MANDATORY allergen labeling required when total SO₂ ≥ 10 mg/kg or 10 mg/L. Maximum levels vary widely by food type. Temporary group ADI for E220-E228.
eu.legal_reference: Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008, Annex II; EU Directive 2003/89/EC (allergen labeling)

usa.notes: MANDATORY allergen declaration 'contains sulfites' required when ≥10 ppm SO₂. Banned from fresh fruits and vegetables intended to be eaten raw (since 1986).

japan.japanese_regulation_name: 亜硫酸水素ナトリウム
japan.notes: Part of sulfite group; allergen labeling required

canada.notes: Allergen labeling required; sulfites are priority allergens in Canada

safety_evaluations.jecfa_adi: 0–0.7 mg/kg bw/day (temporary group ADI for E220-E228, expressed as SO₂)
safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: 0.7 mg SO₂ equivalents/kg bw/day (temporary group ADI for E220-E228)

regulatory_history.event: FDA bans sulfites from fresh fruits and vegetables after several deaths from severe allergic reactions
EU Directive 2003/89/EC makes sulfite allergen labeling mandatory when ≥10 mg/kg or mg/L
EFSA and JECFA re-evaluate sulfites; establish temporary group ADI of 0.7 mg SO₂/kg bw/day with concerns for high consumers

adi_context.source_note: Based on EFSA/JECFA temporary group ADI for sulfites (E220-E228). WARNING: MAJOR ALLERGEN. Affects ~1% general population, 5-10% of asthmatics. Symptoms: wheezing, difficulty breathing, skin rashes, hives, headache, nausea, diarrhea, hypotension, anaphylaxis (rare). EFSA noted safety concerns for high consumers who may exceed ADI through diet alone. Mandatory 'contains sulfites' labeling when ≥10 mg/kg or mg/L.
